message:
fix(core): PreparedStatement::execute_with_params now works for DML

Previously, calling execute_with_params() on a prepared INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
would fail because it delegated to query_with_params(), which rejects DML
statements. Users had to use the less intuitive
Connection::execute_prepared_with_params() instead.

Now PreparedStatement stores a reference to its parent Connection and
detects DML statements in execute() and execute_with_params(), routing
them through the Connection's full execution pipeline (triggers,
constraints, autocommit).

PreparedStatement gains a lifetime parameter 'conn tied to the Connection.

Closes #13

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
